text:
The speed of sound in air depends on a number of factors - air temperature and pressure.  Generally the higher the altitude the lower the speed of sound.

In air, sound waves travel at around 330 metres/second (when the air temperature is 0 degrees centigrade). At 40 degrees centigrade (a very very hot day) the velocity increases to 354 metres/second in air.

The average figure for speed of sound at standard temperature and pressure is quoted as 343 metres per second or 767 miles per hour.

text:
Waterproof really means water-repelling or water-hating. The scientific term for water-hating materials is hydrophobic. 

The opposite of water-hating (hydrophobic) is water-loving (hydrophilic).  

Oils and waxes are common substances that are water-hating or hydrophobic.   Any material that usually absorbs water can be made water-repellent or water-proof if it treated with a waxy or oily substance.

Water-proof containers (example:  some watches, underwater cameras, submarines) are all water-proof because their seams are sealed tightly. Given enough water pressure, however, water may seep into even these water-proof containers.

text:
Probably because it was created by Microsoft!

http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Windows_Metafile

Windows Metafile (WMF) is a common graphics file format on Microsoft Windows systems. It is a vector graphics format which also allows the inclusion of raster graphics. Essentially, a WMF file stores a list of commands that have to be issued to the Windows graphics layer GDI in order to restore the image.

WMF is a 16-bit format introduced in Microsoft Windows 3; a newer 32-bit version with additional commands is called Enhanced Metafile (EMF). EMF is also used as a graphics language for printer drivers.

text:
"The Arrow And The Song" by Henry Wadsworth Longfellow

I shot an arrow into the air,
It fell to earth, I knew not where;
For, so swiftly it flew, the sight
Could not follow it in its flight.

I breathed a song into the air,
It fell to earth, I knew not where;
For who has sight so keen and strong,
That it can follow the flight of song?

Long, long afterward, in an oak
I found the arrow, still unbroke;
And the song, from beginning to end,
I found again in the heart of a friend.

text:
A Sunset

Your love is like a sunset,
wild, bright and true.
The colours blend together,
forming a magical hue.
The day's warm caress
is slipping through the air,
just as though your fingers
would be slipping through my hair.

I sit and watch the sunset,
knowing it soon will end,
thinking of you and
waiting 'til we will be together again.
Slowly now it's going,
coming to a end.
Darkness may be closing in,
but the fun has just begun.

For now I can go home,
go to my darkened room,
lie on the bed and
close my eyes,
fall asleep and dream of you.

some of my best poems happen when I watch a sunset

Sorry it's not easy to draw polynomial division here.
Anyway: the first remainder (if I calculated without errors!) is -a^2-a^3
The remainder of the second division is a^3-2a-3.
You then have the equation:
-a^2-a^3=a^3-2a-3
that is
2a^3+a^2-2a-3=0

You can also write this as
2a^3+a^2=2a+3

Now you can find a graphical solution to the equation by searching the intersection between the cubic y=2t^3+t^2 and the linear y=2t+3, and considering the value of the ascissa of such intersection.

Otherwise, any other method for solving cubic equations can work.

The subnet mask on the subnets should probably be 255.255.255.224 -- 224 is 7 * 32, so this would in theory allow you 8 subnets of 32 computers each.  The subnets would be:

210.115.32.0 - 210.115.32.31
210.115.32.32 - 210.115.32.63
210.115.32.64 - 210.115.32.95
210.115.32.96 - 210.115.32.127

and so on.

There are a few reserved addresses -- I wouldn't use all zeroes or all ones, so in practice, you could use 6 subnets of 30 addresses.  One of your addresses has to be used by the router on the subnet, so you're down to 6 subnets of 29 addresses.

Those addresses sure go fast!

text:
Well, the first question should be "Why is snow white?"  

A beam of white sunlight entering a snow bank is so quickly scattered by a zillion ice crystals and air pockets that most of it comes zinging right back out of the snow bank. No one wavelength is preferentially absorbed or reflected, so snow is essentially the color of the sunlight reflecting off it -- white. 

Then, when the snow melts...it's a clear(ish - depending on the purity of the snow) liquid, and is absorbed into the surroundings, or maybe it evaporates.  Voila!  No more color.

text: To measure the mass-energy of a region of space and say it's zero (with no uncertainty) you'd have to measure it for infinite time. text: Acronym for Point-to-Point Protocol over Ethernet. PPPoE relies on two widely accepted standards: PPP and Ethernet. PPPoE is a specification for connecting the users on an Ethernet to the Internet through a common broadband medium, such as a single DSL line, wireless device or cable modem. All the users over the Ethernet share a common connection, so the Ethernet principles supporting multiple users in a LAN combine with the principles of PPP, which apply to serial connections.

The current salinity of the lake is 40 parts per thousand (ppt).  In comparison, the Dead is 300-400 ppt.

good luck!
